title: buildah commit --squash leaves references to parent image

comments.body: Moving this to buildah instead of buildah.io (the web site).  Should be fixed as of https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3250.
Looks like the PR merged so this is fixed in the main branch.
Is there a tool that would allow me to apply this image info edit before I can access a new release of buildah, to ameliorate the problem early?

If you're not using any features that are specific to the docker format (HEALTHCHECK, ONBUILD, SHELL, HOSTNAME, DOMAINNAME, COMMENT), converting to OCI format somewhere along the way to drop things that the OCI format omits should work, since OCI also doesn't have a "parent" field in its config blob.
One option for doing that is to push to a `dir:` location using `--format oci`, and then to pus  from there to a registry using `skopeo copy`, or by pulling from that location using `buildah` and then pushing the just-pulled image.
push --format oci dir:, pull, tag, push remote with --format v2s2 worked.  I guess I have a workaround.
